title: Home
description: Southwestern Christian College in Terrell, Texas — a Christ-centered college inspiring excellence and building character since 1948.
text: Southwestern Christian College · Terrell, Texas · Est. 1948 Your Future Starts Here. Discover your purpose, earn your degree, and become part of a supportive faith-based community committed to your success. Apply Now Visit Campus Request Information Fall Semester application deadline — July 15 Spring Semester application deadline — December 15 A Legacy of Faith & Education Since 1948, one mission has never changed. Since our founding, SwCC has been dedicated to equipping students with knowledge, character, and leadership grounded in Christian values. Our mission is to inspire lifelong learning, service, and spiritual growth. Southwestern Christian College is accredited by the Commission on Colleges of the Southern Association of Colleges and Schools to award Associate and Baccalaureate degrees. Read Our History How To Apply Two ways to move your future forward. Explore Academic Programs From the Associate of Arts and Associate of Science to the Bachelor of Science in Religious Studies, every SwCC program is built around the development of the whole person — mind, character, and spirit. View All Academic Programs Ready to become a RAM? Apply now and begin your journey toward academic success. Submit Application Explore SWCC Explore Campus Life Academics are only part of the story. Here’s where SwCC students build friendships, faith, and leadership that last well beyond graduation. Student Organizations Athletics Residence Life Chapel & Spiritual Development Leadership Opportunities Community Service Study Abroad Opportunities Future RAM Summer Series Earn Your RAM Pass Before Move-In. Get campus-ready this summer. Meet faculty and staff, complete enrollment requirements, register for classes, receive financial aid guidance, learn about student resources, and connect with your future classmates — all before the semester starts. Earn your RAM Pass Complete important enrollment steps early Receive move-in clearance Prepare for a successful first semester Register Here F

title: Our School
description: Our School at Southwestern Christian College — a small college in Terrell, Texas with a big commitment to academic and spiritual growth.
text: About SwCC Our School A small school with a BIG love of learning — rooted in Terrell, Texas, since 1948. A Message From Our School Welcome to Southwestern Christian College Southwestern Christian College carries deep Christian roots and a historical foundation in Terrell, Texas — and a forward-thinking, dynamic approach to preparing students for what comes next. We hold academic excellence and spiritual growth as equal priorities, taught by dedicated faculty alongside friendly, driven classmates. Our campus sits a short drive from Dallas, the ninth largest city in the country, giving students easy access to internships, culture, and opportunity without leaving behind the close-knit feel of a small college community. Give us a semester, and we’ll help change your path. Give us a year, and watch the evolution — from student to leader, from student to minister, from student to success story. Our Values We nurture academic excellence within a Christian framework, offering programs that help students meet both academic and spiritual goals. Our campus community blends serious educational pursuit with spiritual introspection and a genuine commitment to service. A Commitment to Excellence Our mission is to cultivate Christian-infused education and develop compassionate leaders — empowering students academically, spiritually, and socially for the work ahead of them. Join Us Today Apply Visit Inquire Enroll Contact Phone 972-524-3341 Email info@swcc.edu Address 200 Bowser Circle, Terrell, TX 75160

title: Meet Our President
description: Meet Dr. Ervin D. Seamster, Jr., President and CEO of Southwestern Christian College.
text: Meet Our President Dr. Ervin D. Seamster, Jr. President and CEO, Southwestern Christian College From Student to President More than 25 years ago, Dr. Ervin D. Seamster, Jr. arrived at Southwestern Christian College as a student with limited academic confidence and a GPA that needed work. The faculty and mentors who surrounded him offered encouragement, prayer, and financial support — and turned his academic career around to Dean’s List standing. After graduating from SwCC, Dr. Seamster pursued advanced theological study at Perkins School of Theology and United Theological Seminary. In January 2017, he returned to lead the very institution that had shaped him, stepping into the role of President and CEO. His leadership emphasizes personal transformation, institutional excellence, and Christian values — shaping students into leaders across ministry, business, education, and beyond. “Give us a semester, and change your life. Give us a year — and witness your evolution.” Dr. Seamster was recently honored with the “Spirit of the King” Award from the City of Terrell, recognizing his leadership and dedication to the community. Past Letters From Our President An archive of dated letters from Dr. Seamster spans March 2020 through March 2025, sharing updates and reflections with the SwCC community.

title: Guiding Philosophy
description: The guiding philosophy of Southwestern Christian College: academic instruction rooted in biblical study.
text: About SwCC Guiding Philosophy Academic instruction paired with spiritual development, grounded in biblical study. Southwestern Christian College exists to prepare students for effective and successful Christian living. That purpose shapes every part of campus life: academic instruction runs alongside spiritual development grounded in the daily study of Scripture. Social Competence We develop each student’s ability to engage meaningfully in their community — to express ideas clearly, collaborate well with others, and pursue a vocation that satisfies both calling and craft. Spiritual Foundation Man has a responsibility to God that supersedes all others. Daily biblical instruction is a fundamental part of life at SwCC, not an elective add-on to it. Integrated Purpose Our academic instruction prepares each student to effectively take their place in the business and social world — well rooted and grounded in the Word of God.

title: Our History
description: The history of Southwestern Christian College, from its 1948 founding as Southern Bible Institute to today.
text: Our History Our History From a forty-five-student Bible institute in Fort Worth to a college campus with deep roots in Terrell, Texas. 1865 The Round House Long before the college existed, the land it now occupies held the “Round House” — the first dwelling built in Terrell. Its octagonal shape was designed for protection, and it held the first glass windows in Kaufman County. It remains one of only twenty surviving round houses in the country. 1948 Southern Bible Institute Is Founded The school opened in the fall of 1948 with about forty-five students in Fort Worth, Texas, under the name Southern Bible Institute. George P. Bowser (1874–1950) stands as a key founding figure in that early work. 1949 A Move to Terrell — and a New Name In the summer of 1949, the Trustees purchased property in Terrell that had previously belonged to Texas Military College. That same ground had earlier served as a co-ed school (1896–1904), a Methodist school, and Colonel Perry’s military academy. With the move, the school took the name it carries today: Southwestern Christian College. 2008 Rebuilding After Fire In the pre-dawn hours of January 27, 2008, a fire destroyed the college’s administration building and an adjacent structure. The campus rebuilt — and the renovation work continues today across the Administration Building, the Student Union, and several residence halls.

Send them to: Southwestern Christian College, P.O. Box 10, Terrell, TX 75160. Step 3 — Other Documents Freshman applicants: official high school transcript(s) Transfer applicants: transcripts from all colleges attended Immunization record (copy of shot records) A current photograph (2x2 preferred) A 600-word typed essay — view the required essay question Financial assistance: complete the FAFSA using SwCC’s school code 003618 Step 4 — Application Fee & Dorm Deposit Application Fee $20, non-refundable Dorm Room Deposit $50, non-refundable Apply Now Ready to submit? title: Scholarships
description: Scholarship opportunities at Southwestern Christian College, from academic and ministerial awards to athletic and music scholarships.
text: Financial Aid Scholarship Opportunities Awards for academics, ministry, music, athletics, and regional legacy — built to recognize the RAMs who earn them. Institutional Scholarships $1,000 Academic Scholarship Per semester, 3.5 GPA or higher. $1,000 Chorus Scholarship Section leaders up to $1,000; non-section leaders $750, per semester. $2,000 Ministerial Scholarship Per semester, for students pursuing ministry and Religious Studies. $2,000 Presidential Scholarship Per semester, 3.7 GPA or higher, for student leaders. Special & Regional Scholarships $2,000 Abraham & Nancy Jones Gray For residents of Alabama or the District of Columbia. $2,000 Bay Workers Memorial Fund For residents of the Bay Area or Northern California. $2,000 Legacy Scholarship For first- or second-generation family members of SwCC graduates. $2,000 Annie Mae Davis Endowed For first- or second-generation family members of SwCC graduates. $4,000 Charles D. Gaskin Ministerial For aspiring ministerial students. $2,000 J.S. Winston Sound Doctrine For aspiring ministerial students. $4,000 Local Incentive Scholarship For residents of Terrell and the surrounding area. Athletic & Program Scholarships Athletic Scholarships For men’s and women’s basketball, volleyball, and track, per NJCAA eligibility. Grants in Aid $1,000 academic grants for incoming freshmen and sophomores with a 3.70 GPA; a $300/semester Presidential Incentive discount for a 3.0 GPA. Program Scholarships Available for Criminal Justice and Interdisciplinary Studies students. Request a Scholarship Application

title: Grants
description: Grant programs available to Southwestern Christian College students, including FSEOG, Texas Equalization Grant, and Pell Grant.
text: Financial Aid Grants Need-based support that doesn’t need to be repaid. Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG) Priority goes to students with the lowest Student Aid Index (SAI) combined with Pell Grant eligibility — those demonstrating exceptional financial need. Texas Equalization Grant (TEG) For Texas residents with demonstrated financial need, enrolled in at least 9 credit hours. Funding is limited, so apply early. Pell Grant For students with exceptional financial need per the FAFSA. Award amount varies with enrolled credit hours, and usage is capped at 12 semesters of enrollment or upon earning a bachelor’s degree. Download Grant Flyer

title: Loans
description: Federal, state, and private loan options for Southwestern Christian College students.
text: Financial Aid Loans Federal, state, and private options when scholarships and grants don’t cover the full cost. Federal Loans Subsidized Requires demonstrated financial need. The government covers accruing interest while you’re enrolled at least half-time, and for the first six months after graduation or withdrawal. Unsubsidized Available regardless of financial need. Interest begins accruing from the date of disbursement. State Loans The Texas College Access Loan (CAL) Program is an alternative loan for students who can’t meet the full cost of attendance after exhausting other aid. Requires at least half-time enrollment and credit approval. Private Loans A supplement when federal aid doesn’t fully cover cost. SwCC directs students to ELM Select, a neutral, non-recommended comparison of private lenders.

title: Work Study
description: The Federal Work-Study Program at Southwestern Christian College — on-campus jobs paired with financial need.
text: Financial Aid Work Study On-campus work experience, paired with the aid you’ve already qualified for. The Federal Work-Study Program A federally funded, need-based aid program that helps cover educational expenses while giving students real on-campus work experience and professional development. Eligibility U.S. citizen, permanent resident, or eligible non-citizen per FAFSA/USCIS standards Demonstrated financial need Satisfactory academic progress, maintained annually Enrolled at least half-time (6+ credit hours) Restrictions Only one work-study position at a time No working during scheduled class or exam times No supervision by relatives Cannot combine work study with another campus job Maximum of 20 hours per week Award Amounts Maximum annual award: $2,000 ($1,000 per term). Earnings cannot exceed the awarded amount. State Work Study SwCC does not currently offer a state-funded work-study program.

title: Academics
description: Academics at Southwestern Christian College — Associate and Bachelor's degree programs built around the whole person.
text: Academics Educating the Whole Person Southwestern Christian College is committed to excellence in Christian education, with a curriculum geared to the development of mind, character, and spirit together. Our Academic Programs Associate of Arts Associate of Science Bachelor of Science in Religious Studies View Degree Plans Associate Degree Requirements Meet all entrance requirements and file a degree plan Earn 62 semester hours, excluding Basic Studies hours Maintain a cumulative GPA of 2.20 Complete all required courses for your degree plan Complete a minimum of 45 semester hours at SwCC Take the ETS Proficiency Profile Exit Exam Satisfy all financial obligations and submit a formal graduation application by the sophomore-year deadline Maintain good moral character and participate in graduation exercises unless excused Bachelor’s Degree Requirements Meet all entrance requirements and file a degree plan Complete a minimum of 53 semester hours of general education Complete a minimum of 78 semester hours required for the B.S. Complete a minimum of 95 semester hours at SwCC (up to 32 hours accepted from a school of preaching or unaccredited institution) Earn at least 131 hours, excluding Basic Studies, with a cumulative GPA of 2.40 Complete two 1-hour internships, after 35 Bible/Religious Education hours and a 2.00 GPA Take the Religious Studies Division Exit Exam and the ETS Proficiency Profile Exit Exam Satisfy all financial obligations and submit a formal graduation application by the senior-year deadline Explore Further Degree Plans Online Classes Academic Catalogs Financial Aid

title: Degree Plans
description: Degree plans and graduation requirements for Associate and Bachelor's programs at Southwestern Christian College.
text: Academics Degree Plans Graduation requirements for every program of study at SwCC. Associate of Arts — 62 Hours Required coursework spans Bible, Computer Science, English, Foreign Language, History, Humanities, Mathematics, Physical Education, Psychology/Sociology, and Speech, plus electives. Associate of Science — 62 Hours Mirrors the Associate of Arts, substituting Natural Science coursework for the Foreign Language requirement. Religious Studies Bachelor of Science — 131 Hours Builds on the associate-level foundation with specialized coursework in Religion, Bible, and Ministry, an internship component, a minor, and additional electives. See Full Course Catalogs

title: Institutional Effectiveness
description: The Office of Institutional Effectiveness at Southwestern Christian College supports data-informed decision making and accreditation.
text: Initiatives The Office of Institutional Effectiveness Data-informed decision making that leads to institutional improvement and enhanced student learning. The Office of Institutional Effectiveness is a team of professionals who support the college-wide effort to promote data-informed decision making. The office assists the campus with accreditation, assessment, analysis, training, and information dissemination. Institutional Effectiveness means the systematic collection, analysis, organization, warehousing, and dissemination of quantitative and qualitative information concerning the characteristics and performance of the institution. Our Continuous Improvement Cycle Define and revise strategic goals Develop and refine assessment measures Collect and analyze data Leverage results for enhancements Three Strategic Goals Academic Excellence Knowledge, intellectual growth, and scholarly work across the liberal arts, including a stronger first-year experience and writing culture. Access and Affordability Recruiting diverse, talented students and expanding opportunity through recruitment and financial aid initiatives. A Thriving and Inclusive College Community Student well-being programs, diverse hiring, and long-term athletics planning.

title: CARES Act Reporting
description: CARES Act HEERF emergency student aid reporting for Southwestern Christian College.
text: Initiatives CARES Act — Reporting In accordance with Section 18004(e) of the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act (“CARES Act”), Southwestern Christian College discloses the following. SwCC signed the Certification and Agreement under Section 18004(a)(1) to receive HEERF funds, including HEERF III (CRRSAA) funds, of which 50% is designated for emergency student grants. The College received $437,977.00 in funding from the U.S. Department of Education to award emergency financial aid grants to students. No awards were made as an emergency financial aid grant for the quarter of January 1, 2024 through March 31, 2024. Eligibility covers domestic undergraduate, degree-seeking students enrolled in Spring 2024 with demonstrated need per their FAFSA Expected Family Contribution. Funds may be used for housing, food, health care, counseling, childcare, and tuition. Quarterly HEERF Reports Reports are published quarterly and available upon request from the Business Office, covering each quarter from September 30, 2020 through the current reporting period.

title: Title IX
description: Title IX non-discrimination policy, reporting contacts, and resources at Southwestern Christian College.
text: Initiatives Title IX of the Education Amendments of 1972 Non-Discrimination Statement Southwestern Christian College is committed to providing a workplace and educational environment, and other benefits, programs, and activities, that are free from sex discrimination. This commitment extends to race, color, national or ethnic origin, religion, age, veteran status, disabilities, and sexual orientation. “No person in the United States shall, on the basis of sex, be excluded from participation in, be denied the benefits of, or be subjected to discrimination under any program or activity receiving Federal financial assistance.” Amnesty Policy Good-faith reporters of gender-based violence are not sanctioned for related alcohol or drug conduct violations. Report an Incident Incidents can be reported to the Title IX Coordinator through the SwCC Incident Report Form, or directly to Campus Police. Reporting Contacts SwCC Campus Police (24/7) (682) 800-2408 or ext. 131 Terrell Police Department (469) 474-2700 Women’s Center of Terrell (972) 563-5555 Domestic Violence Hotline (214) 941-1991 Crisis Center (903) 454-9999

title: Student Achievement
description: Student achievement data and IPEDS reporting for Southwestern Christian College.
text: Student Achievement Student Achievement A holistic educational program that motivates students to value and achieve academic excellence within a commitment to moral and spiritual values. Our mission is to offer a holistic educational program that motivates our students to value and achieve academic excellence within the context of commitment to moral and spiritual values — and to prepare future leaders in distinct communities and the world at large. IPEDS Reports Southwestern Christian College reports student achievement data annually to the Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS). 2021–2024 reports are available from the Office of Institutional Effectiveness upon request. title: Default Prevention
description: Student loan default prevention resources at Southwestern Christian College.
text: Consumer Information Default Prevention The Default Prevention Department wants to help you bring your account current. Borrower Responsibilities Repay your loan regardless of employment or completion status Notify your lender before your due date if you’re unable to pay Make minimum monthly payments on time Notify your lender within 10 days of an address, name, or employment change Notify your lender within 10 days of a transfer or enrollment change Consequences of Default (270+ Days Past Due) Immediate full balance due Credit bureau reporting Federal tax refund seizure and wage garnishment Ineligibility for future financial aid Collection agency referral or civil suit, with borrower responsible for collection costs and attorney fees Possible bar on state professional license renewal Borrower Options Deferment (economic hardship, in-school, unemployment, or disability) Forbearance (mandatory or economic hardship) Consolidation Alternative payment plans (income-sensitive, graduated, or lowered payment) Default Prevention Department Phone (972) 524-3341 ext. 135 Fax (972) 563-7133 Toll Free 1-800-925-9357

title: Policy Statements
description: Intellectual Property Policy for Southwestern Christian College.
text: Consumer Information Policy Statements Intellectual Property Policy Southwestern Christian College supports the development, production, and dissemination of intellectual property by its faculty, staff, and students — including computer programs, literary works, musical works, and distance-education course development. Student-Created IP Work created by students remains the sole and exclusive property of the student author or inventor, except where the student voluntarily transfers that property to the college, or where the work was created as part of assigned college employment. Staff-Created IP Work tied to job duties is the sole and exclusive property of the college. Staff retain rights to work created outside their job duties or through independent professional development. Teaching Materials Syllabi, assignments, and tests remain faculty property, though the college retains the right to use them internally. Faculty must preserve institutional rights when transferring copyright to outside publishers. Governance The Faculty Senate administers and monitors these standards, conducts periodic review of the policy, and resolves related disputes.

title: Our Services
description: Campus services at Southwestern Christian College: campus safety, the Center for Student Success, and more.
text: Consumer Information Our Services Safety, Security, and Supervision is our priority. Campus Safety 24/7 campus police, TCOLE-certified since 2018. Center for Student Success Tutorials, counseling, and workshops. Doris Johnson Library Over 26,000 volumes and growing. Default Prevention Helping students keep loans out of default. Student Support Services Federally funded tutorials, counseling, and structured learning support.

title: Doris Johnson Library
description: The Doris Johnson Library at Southwestern Christian College holds over 26,000 volumes in the Hogan-Steward Learning Center.
text: Consumer Information Doris Johnson Library Named for the college’s head librarian, housed in the Hogan-Steward Learning Center. The Doris Johnson Library sits west of the campus entrance, in a facility built in 1974 and expanded by 1,800 square feet in a 1988 renovation — 5,000 square feet in total. The collection contains over 26,000 volumes, with subscriptions to more than 190 nationally recognized magazines, journals, and newspapers, alongside microforms, CD-ROMs, and electronic databases. Special collections include extensive Bible and Religious Studies holdings and an African American Studies Collection reflecting SwCC’s HBCU heritage. Services Online library catalog Library orientation programs Internet access & online databases Microfiche/microfilm reader & printer Books on tape

title: Center for Student Success
description: The Center for Student Success at Southwestern Christian College offers tutorials, counseling, and workshops.
text: Consumer Information Center for Student Success A place where amazing things get done. The Center for Student Success offers the skill sets essential for earning a college degree — note-taking instruction, study skills training, time management coaching, test-taking strategies, structured motivational learning experiences, and peer tutoring. Director Name Kecia Baker Phone (972) 524-3341 Email Kecia.Baker@swcc.edu Location Science Building

title: Campus Safety
description: Campus Safety and Police at Southwestern Christian College — Clery Act reporting and emergency contacts.
text: Consumer Information Campus Safety Safety, Security, and Supervision is our priority. The SwCC Campus Police Department was approved by the State of Texas and activated on October 4, 2018 through the Texas Commission on Law Enforcement (TCOLE). All campus police officers are TCOLE-certified and commissioned by the department, serving as the primary law enforcement agency responsible for the care, custody, and control of SwCC students, staff, and supporters. In accordance with the Jeanne Clery Disclosure of Campus Security Policy and Campus Crime Statistics Act of 1998, the Campus Police Department keeps a rolling 60-day crime log of all reported incidents on or near college property. Annual Security & Fire Safety Reports 2023 Report 2022 Report 2021 Report 2020 Report SwCC Campus Police Phone 972.524.3341 ext. 131 24/7 Line 682.800.2408 Email SwCCPD@SwCC.edu Chief of Police Dr. Yalonda Coats, PhD Location Timothy Hunt Science Building, 1st Floor Anonymous Reporting Portal

title: Transcript Request
description: Request an official or unofficial transcript from Southwestern Christian College.
text: Registrar Transcript Request Current and former students may request transcripts as long as there is no outstanding college debt or federal loan default. The Registrar’s Office processes requests three to five business days after receipt — older records may take longer. Submit your request at least two weeks before any deadline. Current students can access free unofficial transcripts anytime through Populi; former students can order through the same portal. Request a Transcript Mailed Copy $3 per transcript Emailed Copy $2 per transcript If a transcript request is denied for any reason, the transcript fee is forfeited. Registrar Email Registrar@swcc.edu Mailing Address Southwestern Christian College, Office of the Registrar, PO Box 10, Terrell, TX 75160 Holds on Your Account Balance questions: Business Office, ext. 202 Loan default questions: Financial Aid Debt Prevention Manager, ext. 204

title: Privacy Policy
description: Privacy Policy for the Southwestern Christian College website.
text: Legal Privacy Policy Southwestern Christian College respects the privacy of visitors to this website. Information submitted through our forms — including admissions inquiries, contact requests, and donations — is used solely to respond to your request and to administer college business. We do not sell or share personal information with third parties outside of our normal educational and administrative operations. For questions about this policy, contact info@swcc.edu .
